content.text: Search the web to tell who is currently the Prime Minister of Canada. Only search for one result. Give only the name with no explanation.
Search the web to tell who is currently the Prime Minister of Canada. Only search for one result. Give only the name with no explanation.

arguments.command: curl -s "https://www.google.com/search?q=current+Prime+Minister+of+Canada" | grep -oP '(?<=<div class="BNeawe deIvCb AP7Wnd">)[^<]+' | head -n 1

content.text: Search the web to tell who is currently the Prime Minister of Canada. Only search for one result. Give only the name with no explanation.

arguments.command: curl -s "https://www.google.com/search?q=current+Prime+Minister+of+Canada" | grep -oP '(?<=<div class="BNeawe deIvCb AP7Wnd">)[^<]+' | head -n 1
curl -s "https://www.google.com/search?q=current+Prime+Minister+of+Canada" | sed -n 's/.*<div class="BNeawe deIvCb AP7Wnd">\([^<]*\).*/\1/p' | head -n 1
curl -s -L "https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Prime_Minister_of_Canada" | grep -A 5 "Prime Minister" | grep -oP '(?<=<a href="/wiki/)[^"]+' | head -n 1 | sed 's/_/ /g'
curl -s -L "https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Justin_Trudeau" | grep -oP '(?<=<h1 class="firstHeading" id="firstHeading" lang="en">)[^<]+'
curl -s -L "https://www.google.com/search?q=who+is+the+Prime+Minister+of+Canada" | grep -i "Justin Trudeau" -o | head -n 1
